After millennia upon millennia upon millennia of tribal rule and primitive mentality, Orcs have finally forged the means to unify their scattered people together under centralized control. They reside almost entirely on a large island separate from the rest of the mainland, with but a few prominent colonies spotted along the shoreline(s) and with several territorial claims further inland. The ‘standard’ Orc make up the majority of the population, with Goblins making up the largest minority and Bugbears making up the smallest minority, the latter residing in the more rural parts of the island nation. Other groups like Ologs make up a majority of government and clerical positions, along with Hobgoblins that lead an army or two of their respective subraces in the military. They eventually dubbed their island-nation ‘Ongoria’.

A gentle breeze blows, rustling the leaves of the palm trees that line the shore. In the distance, a group of dolphins can be seen jumping and playing in the water. The air is warm and filled with the sound of waves crashing against the shore and the calls of seagulls overhead. The sand beneath your feet is soft and cool, and as you walk along the shore, you feel a sense of peace and tranquility wash over you.
